title: Chapter 2: Managing Packages With Npm - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of package.json, npm commands, dependencies, and semantic versioning

questions.question: What command creates a new package.json file with default values without prompting for input?
questions.explanation: npm init -y (or npm init --yes) creates a package.json file with default values without prompting the user for any information. Without the -y flag, npm init will ask interactive questions about the project.

questions.question: In semantic versioning (e.g., 2.5.3), what does the middle number (5) represent?

options.text: Minor version — new features, backward compatible

questions.explanation: Semantic versioning follows MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH format. The minor version (middle number) is incremented when new functionality is added in a backward-compatible manner. Major is for breaking changes, and patch is for backward-compatible bug fixes.

questions.question: What does the tilde (~) prefix mean in a dependency version like "~1.3.8"?

options.text: Allow updates to the most recent major version
Allow updates to the most recent patch version (1.3.x)
Allow updates to the most recent minor version

questions.explanation: The tilde (~) allows patch-level updates. ~1.3.8 means npm can install any version >=1.3.8 and <1.4.0. It locks the major and minor versions but allows the latest patch. This is more conservative than the caret (^).

questions.question: What does the caret (^) prefix mean in a dependency version like "^1.3.8"?

options.text: Allow updates to the most recent major version
Allow updates to the most recent patch version only
Allow updates to the most recent minor and patch versions (1.x.x)

questions.explanation: The caret (^) allows minor and patch updates. ^1.3.8 means npm can install any version >=1.3.8 and <2.0.0. It locks only the major version. The caret is the default prefix npm uses when installing packages.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'dependencies' and 'devDependencies' in package.json?

options.text: There is no difference; they are aliases for the same thing
dependencies are for the browser, devDependencies are for Node.js
dependencies are needed at runtime, devDependencies are only needed during development and testing
dependencies are installed globally, devDependencies are installed locally

questions.explanation: dependencies are packages required for the application to run in production (e.g., Express, React). devDependencies are packages only needed during development and testing (e.g., testing frameworks, linters, build tools). devDependencies are not installed when NODE_ENV=production.

questions.question: Which field in package.json specifies the entry point file of a Node.js package?
questions.explanation: The "main" field in package.json specifies the entry point of the package. When someone requires your package, Node.js loads the file specified in the "main" field. It defaults to index.js if not specified.

questions.question: What command installs a package and saves it as a devDependency?
questions.explanation: npm install package-name --save-dev (or -D) installs the package and adds it to the devDependencies section of package.json. Since npm 5, --save is the default for regular dependencies, so the flag is optional for production deps.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the package-lock.json file?

options.text: It prevents anyone from modifying package.json
It locks the exact versions of all installed dependencies to ensure reproducible builds
It encrypts the package.json file for security

questions.explanation: package-lock.json records the exact version of every installed dependency, including nested dependencies. This ensures that every developer and CI/CD environment installs identical dependency trees, preventing "works on my machine" issues.

questions.question: How do you define a custom npm script called "test" in package.json?
questions.explanation: Custom commands are defined in the "scripts" object in package.json. The "test" script is a special lifecycle script that can be run with 'npm test' (no 'run' keyword needed). Custom scripts are run with 'npm run script-name'.

questions.question: Which field in package.json provides a human-readable description of the project that appears in npm search results?
questions.explanation: The "description" field in package.json is a string that describes the package. It is displayed in npm search results and helps users discover your package. It should be a concise summary of what the package does.
